Overall Comments:
Klaudyna came across as a confident and capable candidate — easy to talk to, open to feedback in the room, and clearly someone who has done the real work, not just advised on it. The combination of hands-on AI delivery and consulting experience is appreciated. Overall a positive impression and worth progressing.
**Key Observations**
**(+) Strong AI and consulting profile.** She has built actual LLM-based solutions — not just run workshops — and has the consulting background to sit comfortably with senior stakeholders. That combination is uncommon and directly relevant.
**(+) Natural and composed.** The conversation flowed well and she handled the unexpected presentation request with good composure. The intro was improvised and landed professionally.
**(+) Presents, doesn't read.** She paraphrased rather than reciting slides, which made the delivery feel confident and client-ready.
**(+) Slides have structure and logic.** The deck had a clear thread and so-what oriented titles — a good sign for version one, with room to develop further.
**(+) Strong synthesis of client pain points.** The summary of GNP challenges was well-framed and grounded in quotes. Shows she knows how to listen and distil.
**(+) Receptive to feedback.** Took comments well and engaged constructively in the moment.
**(-) No executive summary slide.** The deck jumped straight into the content without pulling the key message upfront. A single strong opening slide would have made the whole thing land faster and sharper.
**(-) Limited use of structured communication frameworks.** She showed some natural instinct for structure but has limited familiarity with SCM, the Pyramid Principle, and MECE thinking. It showed in how the slides were built.
**(-) Only two slides were really working.** The rest of the deck had potential but didn't fully deliver. An exec summary, a risks slide, and an action plan would have rounded it out.
**Recommendation**
Proceed to next stage. The candidate shows more capability than the slides demonstrate — the thinking is there, the delivery is there, the experience is genuine. The main development area is structured communications: she should get familiar with exec summary conventions, SCM, MECE, and the Pyramid Principle before the next round. That's the one thing worth probing and testing directly next time.
What to test in next interview?: The main development area is structured communications: she should get familiar with exec summary conventions, SCM, MECE, and the Pyramid Principle before the next round. That's the one thing worth probing and testing directly next time.

Piotr came across as a polished and professional candidate — easy to talk to, well prepared, and someone who clearly takes the work seriously. The presentation showed good instincts and a natural delivery style. He also sent a thoughtful thank you note after the meeting, which is a small thing but says something. Overall a positive impression and worth progressing.
Key Observations
(+) Strong opening and close. The intro was improvised after a gentle nudge and was professional and well-framed. The wrap-up landed the context and next steps cleanly. Good composure under light pressure.
(+) Structured and logical. Challenges and next steps were well connected and easy to follow. He thinks in a structured way and it showed on the slides.
(+) Good client instincts. He used GNP language naturally and picked up the ADAPTOVATE slide theme and colour palette unprompted. Small things that show he pays attention.
(+) Fluent delivery. He presented rather than read, which made a real difference to how the content landed.
(+) Humble and receptive. Reacted well to feedback in the room and followed up professionally afterwards.
(-) No exec summary. Key insights were scattered across the deck rather than pulled together upfront. One strong opening slide would have made the whole structure land faster.
(-) Slide titles too descriptive. They named the topic rather than carrying the insight. The so-what needs to be in the title.
(-) Opening on problems felt abrupt. A brief positive framing before the challenges would have made the entry smoother.
(-) Quotes used inconsistently. Effective when they appeared but not systematic enough to build a real thread.
Recommendation
Proceed to next stage. The main thing to test is whether he can apply pyramid structure end-to-end — specifically SCR: can he tell the full story in a few sentences at the top and then expand into the detail? That's the one thing worth probing directly next time.
What to test in next interview?: The main thing to test is whether he can apply pyramid structure end-to-end — specifically SCR: can he tell the full story in a few sentences at the top and then expand into the detail? That's the one thing worth probing directly next time.

What was good / strengths
* Extensive and highly relevant consulting experience across a range of boutique consultancies, with exposure to business development, proposals, solution design, workshop facilitation and delivery.
* Demonstrated strong commercial awareness and a clear understanding of how consulting engagements create value for both clients and the business.
* Significant experience supporting the growth of smaller organisations, which aligns well with the environment and expectations of the role.
* Ambitious, driven and motivated by both personal growth and contributing to business growth.
* Provided strong examples throughout the interview, demonstrating both breadth and depth of experience.
* Particularly impressive in his ability to articulate stakeholder challenges. Rather than simply describing situations, he clearly explained the underlying motivations, drivers and behaviours of both himself and the stakeholders involved.
* His background in psychology was evident in the way he approaches stakeholder engagement, adapts his style and navigates complex situations.
* Demonstrated strong thinking around ROI, value creation and commercial outcomes.
Case study strengths
* A very strong and well-structured presentation that remained within the requested format and slide limit.
* Professional slide design, formatting and branding throughout.
* Included a clear executive summary upfront, providing a concise overview of key findings and recommendations.
* Strong use of evidence and data to support recommendations.
* The “cost of inaction” slide was particularly effective, using relevant statistics to create a compelling case for change.
* Evidence was presented in a highly credible way, combining quantitative insights (e.g. number of interviewees identifying a theme) with qualitative quotes to bring the findings to life.
* Included benchmark data to demonstrate potential outcomes and value that could be achieved, strengthening the overall business case.
* Demonstrated strong critical thinking by identifying AI as a potential opportunity despite it not being explicitly referenced in the case study materials. This showed an ability to look beyond the information provided and identify potential gaps, opportunities and future considerations.
Development areas
Case study
* Whilst the case for change, evidence base and recommendations were all strong, the presentation did not include a clear delivery approach or implementation roadmap.
* There was limited discussion around how the recommendations would be translated into action, including sequencing, governance, delivery structure or practical execution considerations.
Overall
A very strong candidate who demonstrated a high level of consulting capability, commercial awareness and stakeholder understanding. The quality of both the interview responses and case study was among the strongest seen, particularly in relation to evidence-based thinking, communication and insight generation. The primary area to explore further in future stages is the ability to translate strategic insight and recommendations into a practical delivery approach and execution plan.
What to test in next interview?:
Development areas as mentioned above. 
* Whilst the case for change, evidence base and recommendations were all strong, the presentation did not include a clear delivery approach or implementation roadmap.
* There was limited discussion around how the recommendations would be translated into action, including sequencing, governance, delivery structure or practical execution considerations.

Overall Comments:
What was good / strengths
* Strong and relevant consulting experience, communicated clearly and confidently throughout the interview.
* Provided good examples of adapting her approach to manage stakeholder challenges and build alignment, including an example that resulted in follow-on client work.
* Demonstrated strong listening skills, thoughtful responses and effective communication.
* Showed good self-awareness around career aspirations and motivations, with positive and credible reasons for seeking progression and further challenge.
* The case study was well presented, with professional formatting and good attention to detail.
* Built a compelling case for change, supported by relevant evidence and a clear rationale for why action was required.
* Demonstrated a structured approach to delivery, including consideration of timelines, outcomes and implementation.
* Actively engaged the audience throughout the presentation and spent time bringing stakeholders on the journey, which helped build confidence in the proposed approach.
* Demonstrated openness to feedback, immediately capturing observations and discussing them constructively.
Development areas
Case study
* Whilst the presentation flowed well, there was no dedicated executive summary slide. The key messages were communicated verbally, however an executive summary would have strengthened the overall structure and provided a clearer opening narrative.
* The presentation could have been further condensed. The content itself was strong, but several points could have been synthesised into fewer slides to create a more concise and executive-level story.
* Greater information synthesis would have improved the overall impact, particularly given the limited time and the seniority of the audience.
Overall
A strong candidate who demonstrated relevant consulting experience, effective stakeholder management skills and good communication throughout. The case study showed solid thinking, a clear rationale for change and a practical delivery approach. The main development area relates to executive-level communication and synthesis, however these were relatively minor points within an otherwise strong interview. We would recommend progressing to the next stage.
What to test in next interview?:
As mentioned above in development areas
* Whilst the presentation flowed well, there was no dedicated executive summary slide. The key messages were communicated verbally, however an executive summary would have strengthened the overall structure and provided a clearer opening narrative.
* The presentation could have been further condensed. The content itself was strong, but several points could have been synthesised into fewer slides to create a more concise and executive-level story.
* Greater information synthesis would have improved the overall impact, particularly given the limited time and the seniority of the audience.

Overall Comments:
What was good / strengths
* Strong and relevant consulting background, with experience well aligned to the role requirements.
* Communicated confidently throughout the interview and articulated examples clearly.
* Demonstrated good experience managing stakeholder challenges, alignment and organisational complexity.
* Strong understanding of operating model work and transformation concepts.
* Motivations for looking for a new role were clear, credible and showed a desire for progression and challenge.
* Initial discussion suggested good alignment and overall fit for the role.
* Within the case study, the overall project structure and phased approach were logical. The focus on defining clear objectives and intended outcomes within each phase was particularly strong.
Gaps / development areas
Case study
* The presentation lacked a clear executive summary and overall narrative flow. It moved directly into the solution without first establishing the context, key challenges or rationale.
* Whilst supporting evidence was included later in the presentation, it was not clearly connected back to the proposed solution. The relationship between the four evidence areas and the three solution workstreams was unclear.
* The presentation flow felt disjointed, moving from solution to evidence rather than building a logical story from problem ? insight ? recommendation ? delivery approach.
* One slide focused heavily on operating model design despite it representing only one of the three proposed workstreams. Greater balance across all three solution areas would have strengthened the presentation.
* Visual presentation was clean and professional, however there was limited use of visuals or synthesis techniques to simplify and communicate complex information.
* The final delivery/process slide was strong conceptually, particularly around phases, objectives and outcomes. Minor improvements around timeline visualisation and presentation format would have enhanced clarity further.
Overall
* Strong candidate with relevant consulting experience and credible stakeholder management capability. However, for a Senior Consultant level role, the case study was not at the standard expected in terms of executive storytelling, synthesis of information, and structured consulting narrative. Whilst the underlying thinking and recommendations were generally logical, the way the story was communicated and presented did not fully demonstrate the level of consulting communication expected for the role.

Little business judgement. Lack of experience in management consulting (and business in general) clearly visible.
All of her experience presentations focused about process related tasks linked to EU funded projects (applications for grants, satisfying the procedural requirements).
Case not solved. Many of the recommendations not adequate e.g. opening of a new factory or of new offices.
What to test in next interview?: If, then => general business judgement, case structuring.

Overall Comments:
What was good / strengths
* Built rapport well and communicated confidently throughout the interview.
* Strong background and relevant examples that demonstrated credible experience.
* Articulated concepts such as change management, leadership alignment and ROI well.
* Clear experience and understanding within change and people-focused initiatives.
* CV and examples provided aligned well with the experience discussed.
Gaps / development areas
Role alignment
* Discussion around experience, motivation and understanding of the role was heavily focused on change management.
* Whilst change management is an important component of the role, the position requires a broader transformation perspective with stronger strategic and business-led thinking beyond change management alone.
Case study
* No clear executive summary to set out key findings and recommendations.
* Limited use of the data provided to support recommendations and strengthen the overall rationale.
* Recommendations were reasonable, however there was insufficient logical flow between the problem, identified causes and proposed solutions. The “why” behind recommendations was not always clear.
* Limited discussion around implementation considerations, roadmap thinking and anticipated next steps. Greater focus on the “how” would strengthen the approach.
* Some language used (e.g. “burning platform”) felt stronger than expected for a client setting and could create unnecessary concern rather than supporting an evidence-led narrative.
Overall
* Good communication and change management capability. However, concerns around broader transformation capability and consulting thinking required for this role.
